Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to remember what each operator does and the structure of the problem as described

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets start from the beginning and break down each part of the process mapping each to the right operator structure

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Start Arrival A Registration RG Intake Assessment IA Triage Interview TI Triage Classification TC Thats all a sequence right Because each step happens after the previous one So that first big part is a SEQUENCE operator which is of those 5 activities in order

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Wait right sequence can take multiple children right Yeah sequence is all in order

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Next after TC theres an exclusive choice between CE if cardiac or FBP otherwise Thats XOR operator X So S2 X CE FBP Thats straightforward only one of those two happens after the sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then theres the loop part The loop is A B where first A is executed then either exit or do B then A again

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Wait lets look at the loop description Wait maybe I got the loop parts wrong Lets parse that again

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Maybe the part that is repeated is SC then treatment then rediagnostic test

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Lets map the flow when initial test is abnormal Initial test abnormal SC then TM or SPT then FCE or FBA then if abnormal go back to SC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: What if A is SC and B is treatment then rediagnostic test

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Then the execution would be first SC then either exit or run treatment then rediagnostic test then SC again but after SC we have to do the treatment and RDT right

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait so that does not fit because after SC we cannot exit before treatment and RDT

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: What if the loop A is the entire SC then TM or SPT then FCE or FBA and B is tau

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: A tau would mean run SC then treatment then RDT then either exit or run tau and A again which matches repeating the whole consultation treatment and rediagnostic cycle

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That makes sense because the choice to exit or continue is after the RDT result is evaluated

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: But hold on what about the initial test because the initial test is before the loop starts

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After the initial test we need a choice either the result is normal so skip loop or abnormal so execute the loop

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So after the initial test there is an XOR between tau for the normal path and the loop for the abnormal path

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thats exactly the normal path because if the initial test is normal you do nothing and proceed and if abnormal you execute the loop

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After normal results are finally achieved the patient must proceed with advanced imaging AI and nutritional counseling NC in true concurrency

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then there is a final sequence of FAS then DDI

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Before the patient leaves there is another concurrency block with BL ICP and MRF in parallel

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the overall model is a top level sequence of the initial arrival and triage sequence then initial diagnostic XOR then XOR of tau and the treatment loop then parallel AI and NC then FAS then DDI then parallel BL ICP and MRF
